g.text: "The fourth herd made their lair where the deer-trapper and his entourage could not go"
yattha agati nevāpikassa ca nevāpikaparisāya ca tatrāsayaṁ kappayiṁsu
FOUR HERDS OF DEER · CATTĀRO MIGAJĀTĀ
Four Kinds of Ascetics and Brahmins · cattāro samaṇabrāhmaṇā
Ate the bait rushing in, infatuated with it
→ became intoxicated → fell into negligence → trapped
Enjoyed Māra's bait and the baits of the world
rushing in and infatuated → Māra did as he wished
nivāpaṁ nivuttaṁ mārassa amūni ca lokāmisāni
✗ Failed to get free from Māra's power and control
Abstained entirely from the bait — fled to the forest
→ grass and water ran out → extreme emaciation
→ strength and energy failed → returned to the bait
Subsisted on austerities in the forest region:
greens · millet · bark · grass · cow dung · fallen fruits
Bodies emaciated → deliverance of mind lost → returned to that very bait
cetovimutti parihāyi … nivāpaṁ nivuttaṁ mārassa paccāgamiṁsu
✗ Failed to get free from Māra's power and control
Made a lair close to the bait — ate carefully,
not rushing in, not infatuated → not intoxicated
THEN trapper surrounded bait with stake-nets → found
Dwelt near the world's pleasures mindfully,
not infatuated — BUT held speculative views:
"The world is eternal / not eternal / finite / infinite"
"Life force = body / ≠ body" · "Tathāgata after death: exists / does not exist"
✗ Failed to get free — the net of views kept them within Māra's domain
Made lair where the trapper cannot go
Dwelt where Māra and his entourage cannot go
Ate the bait not rushing in, not infatuated
Not intoxicated → not negligent → Māra gave up
yattha agati mārassa ca māraparisāya ca
✓ Got free from Māra's power and control
WHERE MĀRA CANNOT GO · AGATI MĀRASSA
"has blinded Māra, gone beyond the Evil One's sight, becoming invisible to him"
andhamakāsi māraṁ, apadaṁ vadhitvā māracakkhuṁ adassanaṁ gato pāpimato
Secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states
Born of seclusion (vivekajaṁ) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukhaṁ)
Free from reflection and examination (avitakkaṁ · avicāraṁ)
Born of collectedness (samādhijaṁ) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukhaṁ)
With fading of desire for uplifting joy (pītiyā ca virāgā)
Experiencing pleasure with the body (sukhaṁ ca kāyena paṭisaṁvedeti)
Abandoning bodily pleasure and pain (sukhassa ca pahānā · dukkhassa ca pahānā)
Settling of mental pleasure and displeasure (somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā)
Base of Neither Perception Nor Non-Perception
Cessation of Perception and What Is Felt
Having seen with wisdom, his taints are completely exhausted
paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā honti
He has crossed over entanglement in the world
